overview: The Marathon County Board held an adjourned organizational meeting where committee appointments were approved, two at-large executive committee members were elected (Supervisors Hapa and Arstead), and a resolution was passed to reduce landfill tipping fees for disaster-related debris following severe weather that impacted the Ringle area. The board suspended its rules to consider the emergency tipping fee resolution, which passed unanimously.

agenda.item: Pledge of Allegiance and moment of reflection
Roll call and technical difficulties with meeting manager system
Committee appointments presentation and remarks from Chair
Election of at-large executive committee members
Announcement of executive committee election results
Consent agenda items C10 through C14A
Resolution 24-26: Out of home placement reserve increase

discussions.body: Chair Gibbs presented committee appointments developed with Vice Chair Boots, emphasizing goals of increasing perspective diversity, developing new supervisors as leaders, and accommodating supervisor preferences. He noted that over 34% of the board is in their first or second term. The motion by Supervisor Cavelli, seconded by Supervisor Crims, was carried but was not unanimous.

discussions.item: Election of At-Large Executive Committee Members
discussions.body: Three supervisors were nominated for two at-large executive committee positions: Supervisor Hapa (nominated by Supervisor Mask), Supervisor Leur (nominated by Supervisor Rosenberg), and Supervisor Arstead (nominated by Supervisor Boots). Supervisor Rosenberg spoke in support of Supervisor Leur, noting she 'advocates for our county's smallest children and oldest elders.' Paper ballots were distributed and counted. Supervisors Hapa and Arstead were elected to the executive committee.
Items C10 through C14A were approved on consent. Motion by Supervisor Cavelli, seconded by Supervisor Drebeck. The motion carried unanimously.

discussions.item: Resolution 24-26: Out of Home Placement Reserve Increase
discussions.body: Motion by Supervisor Morash, seconded by Supervisor Robinson. The resolution was approved but not unanimously with no discussion.
Motion by Supervisor P, seconded by Supervisor Robinson. The resolution was approved but not unanimously with no discussion.

discussions.item: Resolution 26-26: Landfill Tipping Fees for Disaster Debris
discussions.body: Administrator Leonard and Director Hagen Busher presented the proposal from the Solid Waste Management Board to reduce tipping fees for storm debris following severe weather that caused flooding and tornado damage, particularly in Ringle. The state waived $13.10, Ringle waived $2.33, and the county proposed reducing fees to the commercial rate of approximately $36.94 per ton. Two residents, Jessica Toasty and Matt Haybeck, were granted special dispensation to speak, requesting the county waive all fees. Supervisor Rosenberg requested continued discussion at the Solid Waste Board. The board first voted unanimously to suspend rules to consider the resolution, then approved Resolution 26-26 unanimously.

publicComment: Two residents were granted special permission to speak during the tipping fee discussion. Jessica Toasty requested Marathon County waive all fees for six months, noting that the state, DNR, and Ringle are waiving their fees and that private businesses have donated significant resources. Matt Haybeck spoke on behalf of homeowners whose houses must be demolished, requesting fee waivers for underinsured residents and more time for cleanup since many insurance adjusters have not yet arrived.
actionItems: Committee appointments approved as presented by Chair Gibbs and Vice Chair Boots
Supervisors Hapa and Arstead elected to at-large executive committee positions
Resolution 24-26 approved: out of home placement reserve increase
Resolution 25-26 approved: social services budget amendment
Resolution 26-26 approved: reduced landfill tipping fees for disaster-related debris through June 17th
Administrator Leonard to work with Community Foundation on potential reimbursement mechanism for unreimbursed insurance costs for landowners
Solid Waste Management Board to develop clear policy on handling similar disaster situations within six months
Supervisor Gao requested notification of future Solid Waste Board meetings
title: Marathon County Regular Meeting
